seo.title: Google ADK Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Google ADK agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using Google ADK face heightened compliance risks due to multi-agent systems processing sensitive candidate data across resume screening, interview scheduling, and bias detection agents. Common ADK failure modes like circular dependencies and tool mismatches can lead to inconsistent outputs that violate EEOC guidelines or EU AI Act requirements for high-risk AI systems. Vex provides essential guardrails through before_agent_callback integration to ensure auditable, bias-free hiring decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI hiring tools from discriminating based on race, gender, age, or other protected characteristics; requires evidence of non-discriminatory impact.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Monitors agent outputs for biased language patterns and flags decisions correlating with protected attributes before execution.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates bias audits for automated employment decision tools and public disclosure of audit results.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Automates annual bias audits by logging agent decision traces and generating compliance reports for NYC DCA submission.

complianceRequirements.description: Classifies recruitment AI as high-risk, requiring risk management, data governance, and human oversight.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces human-in-the-loop for high-risk decisions and validates input data quality across multi-agent workflows.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits screening out candidates based on disability-related resume gaps.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Detects and neutralizes proxy discrimination signals like employment gaps or non-traditional career paths.

industryRisks.name: Disparate Impact in Resume Screening
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent systems inadvertently filter out protected groups due to correlated resume features like school names or zip codes.
industryRisks.example: ADK agents reject 80% of HBCU graduates due to untrained 'prestige scoring' tools.

industryRisks.name: Lack of AI Disclosure
industryRisks.description: Candidates not notified when Gemini-powered agents make screening decisions, violating notification laws.
industryRisks.example: Automated rejection emails omit AI usage disclosure required by NYC Local Law 144.

industryRisks.name: Session State Bias Accumulation
industryRisks.description: Long conversation state corruption amplifies bias across interview scheduling and scoring agents.
industryRisks.example: Early gender-biased resume scores influence downstream interview slot assignments.

industryRisks.name: Circular Agent Dependencies
industryRisks.description: Agents endlessly loop between bias checker and scorer, generating inconsistent compliance outputs.
industryRisks.example: Bias detection agent calls scoring agent which recalls bias checker, corrupting audit trails.

implementation.title: Vex Before-Agent Callback for Bias Monitoring
implementation.description: Intercepts all ADK agent calls to validate resume screening inputs/outputs against protected characteristics.

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Circular Dependency Guard
implementation.description: Prevents infinite loops in bias-checker → scorer → interviewer agent chains with session state validation.
implementation.code:
import vex
from google.adk import MultiAgentSystem

@vex.guardrail(max_depth=5, track_sessions=True)
def prevent_circular_deps(agent_call: dict) -> bool:
    session_id = agent_call.get('session_id')
    call_chain = vex.get_session_state(session_id, 'call_chain', [])
    if len(call_chain) > 3 and call_chain[-1] == agent_call['agent_name']:
        return False
    call_chain.append(agent_call['agent_name'])
    vex.set_session_state(session_id, 'call_chain', call_chain)
    return True

mas = MultiAgentSystem(callback=prevent_circular_deps)
mas.add_agent('bias_checker', agent1)
mas.add_agent('scorer', agent2)
mas.run(candidate_data)

implementation.title: AI Disclosure & Audit Logging
implementation.description: Automatically logs all AI decisions with timestamps and generates NYC Law 144 compliance reports.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Google ADK in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
TechCo deployed a Google ADK multi-agent system for high-volume hiring, with Agent A screening 10,000 resumes daily, Agent B scoring cultural fit, and Agent C scheduling interviews. The system used Gemini 1.5 for natural language processing but suffered from circular dependencies where the bias checker agent would re-call the scoring agent, corrupting session state over long evaluation chains. Resume screening disproportionately rejected candidates from certain zip codes correlated with protected classes, while candidates received no AI disclosure in rejection emails.

During an EEOC audit, TechCo discovered the system had filtered out 65% of Hispanic applicants due to untrained 'cultural fit' heuristics. NYC Local Law 144 compliance was impossible without proper audit logs, and EU operations faced high-risk AI Act violations. Without guardrails, the deployment risked $1.5M in fines plus class-action lawsuits.
caseStudyScenario.outcome:
Vex integration via before_agent_callback resolved all failure modes. Circular dependencies were blocked at depth 3, session state corruption eliminated, and bias patterns flagged in real-time with 98% accuracy. Automated disclosures were added to all communications, and annual audit reports satisfied NYC Law 144 requirements.

The system reduced disparate impact by 87%, passed EU AI Act conformity assessment, and maintained diverse hiring pipelines. TechCo avoided regulatory action and gained defensible audit trails for all 50,000 decisions processed quarterly.
